table_description: Customer support tickets — priority, category, resolution time, and satisfaction.
use_cases: Measure support quality (resolution time, satisfaction scores)
Identify customers with high ticket volume (churn risk signal)
Analyze ticket categories to prioritize product improvements
data_quality_notes: resolved_at is NULL for open and in_progress tickets
satisfaction_score is 1-5 but NULL if the customer didn't rate (about 30% unrated)
status values: 'open', 'in_progress', 'resolved', 'closed' — closed means resolved and confirmed by customer
Resolution time = resolved_at - created_at (for both resolved and closed tickets)

table_columns.name: priority
table_columns.description: Priority level: low, medium, high, critical

table_columns.name: category
table_columns.description: Ticket type: billing, technical, feature_request, onboarding

table_columns.name: status
table_columns.description: Current status: open, in_progress, resolved, closed

table_columns.name: resolved_at
table_columns.description: When the ticket was resolved — NULL if open

table_columns.name: satisfaction_score
table_columns.description: Customer satisfaction 1-5 — NULL if not rated
